items.title: Indonesia, Singapore say key oil passage will remain ‘accessible’
items.summary:
Indonesia and Singapore vowed on Monday that the Strait of Malacca, a critical oil transit chokepoint in the region, will remain “accessible” even as Iran imposes fees on ships traversing the Strait of Hormuz.
Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto discussed the matter with Singaporean Prime Minister Lawrence Wong in Jakarta as Southeast Asia reels from the effects of oil prices pushed sky-high by

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Tech Leads Stock Futures Higher; Trump Heads to NATO Summit | Bloomberg Brief 07/06/2026
items.summary: US equity futures rise in a tech rebound as SK Hynix is set for a US listing this week. Oil slumps as flows through the Strait of Hormuz are restored after some tanker U-turns and after OPEC+ members agree to a modest rise in oil supply next month. Presidents Donald Trump and Volodymyr Zelenskyy are expected to meet at a NATO summit in Turkey. Anneka Treon of ING discusses the earnings environment

China dam collapse: hundreds evacuated after typhoon rains breach Guangxi reservoir
items.summary:
Floodwater breached a reservoir in southern China and threatened others, as heavy rain pounded the region, leading to the evacuation of hundreds of people in the area.
Chinese authorities urged caution and called for rescue and evacuation, with bad weather predicted to continue under the influence of Typhoon Maysak.

items.title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rock Kyiv on Eve of NATO Summit

items.title: EU should think twice before weaponising its market against China
items.summary:
For much of the post-Cold-War era, the European Union understood economic interdependence as a source of mutual prosperity. Open markets, free trade and multilateral rules were regarded not merely as economic principles but as the foundations of European normative influence. That assumption is rapidly giving way to a different logic.

items.summary:
Hong Kong’s legislature may review and tighten its code for members in light of the resignation of a lawmaker arrested for alleged drink-driving and failing to report an accident, its president has said.
